DISCOVERY 302, MATTER A R G U E D; AT CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFFS ANGUS BEAN, GENE MAZE, SATYRICON INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, MARIE STRANE'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF RICHARD KAMSON AND FOR MONETARY DISCOVERY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART. REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. FURTHER, COUNSEL IS ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER RE: DEPOSITION DATES. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER. MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-22-2012 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, MATTER A R G U E D; AT CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATION: PLAINTIFFS ANGUS BEAN, GENE MAZE, SATYRICON INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, MARIE STRANE'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED IN PART. GRANTED WITH RESPECT TO DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO PLAINTIFFS' REQUESTS 1, 5, 8, 9, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 22 AND 23. ST. PAUL SHALL REDACT IDENTIFYING INFORMATION AND MEDICAL INFORMATION OF THIRD PARTIES. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL IS ALSO GRANTED WITH RESPECT TO REQUESTS 2, 3, 4, 6, 7 & 19 EXCEPT THAT ANY DOCUMENTS RELATED TO WORKERS' COMPENSATION POLICIES NEED NOT BE PRODUCED. ST. PAUL'S RESPONSES TO REQUESTS 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 22, 23, 24, 25, AND 26 STATE THAT ST. PAUL HAS NOT BEEN ABLE TO LOCATE ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS AFTER A DILIGENT SEARCH, BUT THE RESPONSES ARE NOT VERIFIED AND DO NOT SUPPLY ALL OF THE INFORMATION REQUIRED BY CCP 2031.230 WHEN A RESPONDING PARTY MAKES A REPRESENTATION OF INABILITY TO COMPLY WITH A PARTICULAR DEMAND. ST. PAUL SHALL PROVIDE SUPPLEMENTARY RESPONSES TO THOSE REQUESTS THAT MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF CCP 2031.230 AND ARE VERIFIED PURSUANT TO CCP 2031.250 WITHIN 14 DAYS. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS DENIED WITH RESPECT TO REQUEST NO. 20, BUT PLAINTIFF IS NOT PRECLUDED BY THIS ORDER FROM SEEKING SUCH INFORMATION IF PLAINTIFFS CAN MAKE A SHOWING THAT THEY ARE LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON A CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM ERNST HALPERIN, NOT REPORTED.

Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Deft'S Req For Production Of Docs
Off Calendar Jun-22-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
ALBERT G. STOLL, ESQ., AGS, TEL. 415.576.1500, FOR PLAINTIFFS AND MOVING PARTIES ANGUS BEAN, GENE MAZE, SATYRICON INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, MARIE STRANE.
ROBERT GLENN SEEDS, ESQ., GREENAN, PEFFER, SALLANDER & LALLY, TEL. 925.866.1000, FOR DEFENDANT THE TRAVELLERS INSURANCE COMPANY.

MATTER A R G U E D; AT CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATION: PLAINTIFFS ANGUS BEAN, GENE MAZE, SATYRICON INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, MARIE STRANE'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant's Request For Production Of Documents IS GRANTED IN PART. GRANTED with respect to documents responsive to Plaintiffs' requests 1, 5, 8, 9, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 22 and 23. St. Paul shall redact identifying information and medical information of third parties. Plaintiff's motion to compel is also GRANTED with respect to Requests 2, 3, 4, 6, 7 & 19 except that any documents related to workers' compensation policies need not be produced. St. Paul's responses to Requests 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 22, 23, 24, 25, and 26 state that St. Paul has not been able to locate any responsive documents after a diligent search, but the responses are not verified and do not supply all of the information required by CCP 2031.230 when a responding party makes a representation of inability to comply with a particular demand. St. Paul shall provide supplementary responses to those requests that meet the requirements of CCP 2031.230 and are verified pursuant to CCP 2031.250 within 14 days. Plaintiff's motion is DENIED with respect to Request No. 20, but Plaintiff is not precluded by this order from seeking such information if Plaintiffs can make a showing that they are likely to prevail on a claim for punitive damages. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED.

COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER.

JUDGE PRO TEM ERNST HALPERIN, NOT REPORTED.
